articles.title: UK Plans for Possible Food Shortages
articles.summary: The UK government is making plans in case there might be some food shortages, like chicken and pork, by summer if a war in Iran continues. This is a worst-case scenario, meaning it might not happen. Officials are worried because the war could close a sea route called the Strait of Hormuz and stop the supply of carbon dioxide (CO2), which is used to slaughter animals and keep food fresh. However, government sources say this is just planning, not a prediction, and they don't expect actual shortages. Food leaders are more concerned about prices going up. Stores like Tesco say they have no problems with food availability now. The situation is being watched closely.

articles.title: World Cup Train Tickets Cost Too Much
articles.summary: The World Cup is coming to New Jersey, and fans might have to pay a lot of money to take the train to the games. Usually, a train ticket costs about $13, but for the World Cup, it could cost over $100. This is because of special event pricing. The governor of New Jersey, Mikie Sherrill, says FIFA, the group that runs the World Cup, should help pay for these train rides. She says FIFA is making billions of dollars from the World Cup, but they are not giving any money for transportation. This means fans, including kids and seniors, have to pay the full high price. The stadium in New Jersey will host eight matches, including the final. Governor Sherrill does not want regular train riders in New Jersey to pay for this cost for years. FIFA says they are surprised by her comments and think fans should pay only the normal cost.

articles.title: New Alzheimer's Drugs Might Not Help Much
articles.summary: Scientists studied new drugs for Alzheimer's disease, which affects memory and thinking. The drugs try to clean up sticky stuff in the brain called beta amyloid. Tests showed they can slow down the disease a little, but not enough to make a big difference for patients. The drugs are very expensive, costing £90,000 for a course, and can cause side effects like brain swelling. Some experts say the drugs are not worth it because they don't help enough and are hard to get. Other scientists disagree and think the analysis is wrong. This debate is important for patients and families deciding what to do.

articles.title: Justice Jackson Talks About Court Orders for Trump
articles.summary: Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson spoke at Yale Law School about emergency orders from the court. These orders helped President Trump with policies on immigration and funding, even when lower courts said they might be illegal. Jackson called the orders "scratch-paper musings" and said they don't have good explanations. She thinks this is a problem because lower courts have to follow these quick decisions. Justice Sonia Sotomayor also talked about this issue recently. Jackson has criticized these orders before in her work and with other justices. The event was important because she spoke to the public, not just other judges.

articles.title: FDA Considers Changing Rules on Peptide Injections
articles.summary: The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is planning a meeting this summer to think about making it easier for people to get peptide injections. Peptides are substances that some people use to try to build muscle, heal injuries, or look younger, but they are not approved by the FDA, and there is little research to prove they are safe or work. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has promised to loosen the rules on peptides, and some of his supporters like them too. The FDA will ask outside experts to review seven popular peptides, like BPC-157, to see if they can be made safely by pharmacies. A former FDA official worries this could be dangerous because peptides haven't been tested like other drugs. The FDA had previously said peptides are risky and shouldn't be made by pharmacies, but now they might change their mind.

articles.title: Democrats Fight Against Corruption in Government
articles.summary: House Democrats in Washington, D.C., have started a new group called a task force to fight corruption and make government more honest. They are worried that people are losing trust in the government because some leaders make decisions for their own benefit instead of for Americans. The task force, led by Rep. Joe Morelle, wants to change rules to stop corruption, like banning stock trading for government workers and setting term limits for Supreme Court justices. They are focusing on President Trump's business deals in other countries, saying this shows corruption, but the White House disagrees and says Trump acts in the public's best interest. This effort is part of Democrats' plan to win back control of Congress in the upcoming elections.
